title: What to Know About This Fund's $4 Million Exit From SmartStop Self Storage
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: GSI Capital Advisors fully exited its position in SmartStop Self Storage REIT (SMA) on May 14, 2026, selling 124,919 shares for approximately $4.01 million. Despite the exit, SmartStop's Q1 2026 fundamentals showed improvement with 20% revenue growth and a swing to profitability, though the stock has underperformed the S&P 500 by 38 percentage points over the past year.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: GSI Capital's complete exit suggests loss of confidence in the specific investment, and the stock is down 10% YoY and underperforming the S&P 500 significantly. However, underlying business fundamentals improved with 20% revenue growth, swing to profitability, and management guidance for continued progress in 2026. The exit appears strategic reallocation rather than fundamental deterioration.
GSI Capital's largest holding at 11.4% of AUM ($21.0M), indicating strong confidence in the data center REIT as a core real estate investment.
Second-largest holding at 10.6% of AUM ($19.6M), demonstrating GSI Capital's continued confidence in the healthcare REIT sector.
Third-largest holding at 9.4% of AUM ($17.4M), showing strong conviction in the industrial logistics REIT.
Significant holding at 4.8% of AUM ($8.9M), indicating GSI Capital maintains exposure to data center REITs despite exiting SmartStop.

title: 3 High Dividend Monthly Dividend Stocks Now
author: Bob Ciura
description: The article highlights three monthly dividend stocks with yields above 3%: Savaria Corporation (mobility solutions), SmartStop Self Storage REIT (self-storage properties), and Trinity Capital (BDC providing venture debt financing). All three companies demonstrated strong recent financial performance with revenue growth, improved profitability metrics, and increased dividend payments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Reported 135% year-over-year FFO growth, 2.5% same-store revenue increase, occupancy improved to 92.6%, and FFO per share up 12%. Portfolio expansion and defensive business model in self-storage sector support positive outlook.

Investment income increased 22.3% year-over-year to $75.6 million, NAV per share rose 31.9% year-over-year to $13.31, and net assets from operations reached $27.6 million. Strong origination activity and portfolio performance demonstrate solid financial health.

title: SmartStop Self Storage REIT's (SMA) CEO Buys 6,250 Shares for $198,200
author: Cory Renauer
description: SmartStop Self Storage REIT's CEO H. Michael Schwartz purchased 6,250 shares for $198,187.50 on November 17, 2025, indicating potential confidence in the company's future growth, particularly after acquiring a third-party management platform.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed financial performance with modest 2.5% revenue growth, but rising funds from operations and strategic third-party management platform acquisition suggest potential for future growth
